ingredients.name: Shinrokuen Deer Garden
ingredients.entityContext: The deer garden within Mishima Taisha grounds is home to the sacred deer (shinroku) that inspired Nokotan's design, making it a pilgrimage stop for fans who want to meet the real-world animals behind the show's deer-girl premise.

ingredients.name: Daini Shibetsutenbo Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: 第二しべつ展望パーキング
ingredients.address: Chashikotsu, Shibetsu, Shibetsu District, Hokkaido 086-1622, Japan

ingredients.name: Deer Statue
ingredients.entityContext: A stone deer statue in Nara Park, one of many scattered through the grounds where Nara's famous free-roaming sika deer wander, echoing the deer-themed world Nokotan inhabits and referenced visually in the series.

ingredients.name: Hino Station
ingredients.entityContext: Hino Station on the Keio Takao Line is the main gateway to the show's primary setting, and the station exterior and surrounding streets appear in early episodes as Torako's daily commute route to school.

ingredients.name: Hodokubo Station
ingredients.entityContext: Hodokubo Station on the Tama Monorail is a documented pilgrimage location from the anime, with the distinctive monorail platform and surrounding streets faithfully recreated in background scenes near the school.

ingredients.name: Kinkasan Koganeyama Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: Kinkasan island is famous for its wild sika deer that roam freely around the shrine, a real-world parallel to Nokotan's nature as a deer spirit, and the location is referenced in the anime's supernatural deer lore.

ingredients.name: Kofuku-ji
ingredients.entityContext: Kofuku-ji in Nara Park stands among hundreds of freely roaming sika deer, and the temple grounds appear as a reference backdrop in episodes where the show draws on Japan's deep cultural link between deer and sacred spaces.

ingredients.name: Minamidaira Gymnastics
ingredients.entityContext: The Minamidaira gymnasium in Hino City is a confirmed background location from the anime, appearing in scenes set in the school's neighborhood and recognizable to local fans by its distinctive facade.

ingredients.name: Mishima Taisha
ingredients.entityContext: Mishima Taisha is one of Japan's few shrines that keeps sacred deer (shinroku) on its grounds, and the shrine's deer garden is directly tied to Nokotan's character origin as a deer deity descended from shrine animals.

ingredients.name: Muntjac Breeding Facility
ingredients.entityContext: This facility on Hachijojima breeds kyon (Chinese muntjac), the exact species of deer that Nokotan is based on, making it the most species-accurate pilgrimage spot in the franchise and a fan favorite for photo opportunities.

ingredients.name: Nakahodokubo Bridge
ingredients.description: Nakahodokubo Bridge (中程久保橋) — local bridge in Tama, Tokyo, near Lawson and Hidakaya. Not listed on Google Maps, but the coordinates fall in this area — a verified scene location. Navigate by coordinates / Street View.
ingredients.entityContext: Nakahodokubo Bridge is a closely verified background location from the series, appearing in scenes where Torako and Nokotan walk through the Hino residential streets, with the surrounding convenience store and fast-food shop visible in the shot.

ingredients.name: Near the Nikko Yumoto Rest House
ingredients.localName: 日光湯元レストハウス付近
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店

ingredients.name: Noboryo-Toge Observation Deck
ingredients.entityContext: The Noboryo-Toge pass on Hachijojima offers a panoramic view of the island's volcanic landscape, and the locale appears in scenes set on the island during the arc exploring Nokotan's connection to the kyon breeding environment.

ingredients.name: Ruins of Kofuku-ji Chumon (Central Gate)
ingredients.address: 中門, Omuroouchi, Ukyo Ward, Kyoto, 616-8092, Japan

ingredients.name: Shikagahara
ingredients.entityContext: Shikagahara (Deer Plain) in Miyazaki is a highland meadow whose name and sweeping open landscape echo the natural habitat associated with Nokotan, referenced as part of the anime's broader exploration of deer-connected sacred places across Japan.

ingredients.name: Shikazono
ingredients.entityContext: Shikazono (Deer Meadow) near Yahiko Shrine in Niigata is a small deer enclosure historically associated with the shrine, referenced in the anime in the context of the spiritual bond between deer and Japanese shrine culture.

ingredients.name: Soshukasuga Shrine
ingredients.address: 700 Matanocho, Totsuka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 245-0066, Japan

ingredients.name: Todai-ji
ingredients.entityContext: Todai-ji is set within Nara Park, home to Japan's most famous free-roaming sika deer, and the Great Buddha Hall grounds appear as a visual reference in the series when invoking the centuries-old Japanese tradition of deer as sacred messengers.

ingredients.name: Yahiko Shrine
ingredients.entityContext: Yahiko Shrine in Niigata appears alongside its adjacent Shikazono deer enclosure in the anime, and the shrine's forested approach path is referenced in episodes dealing with Nokotan's background as a spirit connected to shrine deer.

ingredients.name: Yahikoyama Panorama Tower
ingredients.localName: 弥彦山 パノラマタワー
ingredients.address: 2898 Yahiko, Nishikan Ward, Yahiko-mura, Nishikanbara-gun, Niigata 959-0323, Japan

ingredients.name: Yunoko
ingredients.entityContext: Yunoko (Lake Yuno) near Nikko Yumoto appears in scenes set around the Nikko highland area, with the lake's misty shore serving as background for a quiet character moment between Torako and Nokotan during the Nikko excursion arc.
